name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
summary: Day 76-77 quiet — UKMTO and Lloyd's List confirm no Houthi attacks on commercial shipping in Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb / Gulf of Aden during Iran war's 2026 phase; UKMTO maintains 'moderate' (not critical) for Bab el-Mandeb. **NEW NAVAL GEOMETRY: France's Charles de Gaulle CSG transited Suez Canal toward Red Sea / Gulf of Aden following CMA CGM San Antonio strike — first European naval-presence shift of cycle.** Paris described the move as a 'defensive step in full respect of international law' to 'reassure regional partners and support maritime security.' Deployment is separate from but parallel to the conditional UK-French joint Hormuz mission; ABC reports French CSG specifically moved south of Suez 'ahead of conditional UK-French Hormuz mission.' Container freight FBX: Asia-USWC ~$2,127/FEU; Med ~$3,850/FEU (+15% week — routing-penalty intact, Med-route elevation reflects Hormuz/Suez dynamic). The Houthi non-response across May 4 kinetic + May 5 pause + May 6 MOU framework continues to validate Soufan coordination-decay thesis.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea

recent_signals.headline: FRANCE CHARLES DE GAULLE CSG TRANSITED SUEZ TO RED SEA / GULF OF ADEN FOLLOWING CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO STRIKE — FIRST EUROPEAN NAVAL SHIFT OF CYCLE; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Iranian President Pezeshkian (CONFIRMED — French armed forces, Naval News, ABC/NBC, US News, Washington Times). Paris: 'defensive step in full respect of international law' to 'reassure regional partners and support maritime security.' Day 76-77 of Houthi quiet sustained. FBX Med ~$3,850/FEU (+15% week — routing-penalty intact).
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED — French CSG redeployment multi-source (French primary + Western corroboration); Houthi quiet = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO, Lloyd's List)

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Houthis entered the war with a missile at Israel on March 28 but have NOT attacked Red Sea shipping (yet)

13 signals. Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | Day 35: Still zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | Day 38 of war — zero confirmed Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks
